title: GraphQL API rate limit structure changed
raw_changelog: Linear GraphQL API now uses complexity-based rate limiting instead of request count.
before_text:
## Rate Limits

### GraphQL API
- 1500 requests per hour
- 250 requests per minute burst
- Headers: X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
after_text:
## Rate Limits

### GraphQL API
- 10,000 complexity points per hour
- 1,000 complexity points per minute burst
- Simple queries: ~1-5 points
- Nested queries: ~10-50 points
- Headers: X-RateLimit-Complexity-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Complexity-Reset

**Note:** The old request-count headers are no longer returned.
diff_text:
--- before
+++ after
@@ -3,4 +3,8 @@
 ### GraphQL API
-- 1500 requests per hour
-- 250 requests per minute burst
-- Headers: X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
+- 10,000 complexity points per hour
+- 1,000 complexity points per minute burst
+- Simple queries: ~1-5 points
+- Nested queries: ~10-50 points
+- Headers: X-RateLimit-Complexity-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Complexity-Reset
+
+**Note:** The old request-count headers are no longer returned.

ground_truth.breaking_reason: Rate limit headers changed. Code parsing old headers will break. Complex queries may hit limits sooner.
ground_truth.action_required: Update rate limit handling code to use new complexity-based headers. Review complex queries that may consume many points.
ground_truth.confidence_notes: Breaking change in rate limit mechanism and headers
